mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals imminent recession risk, forcing downward revisions to corporate earnings estimates and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal exhibits no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in housing construction and consumer credit expansion confirms robust aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year rolling window, or individual series outperform the joint signal in predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, rising industrial production reflects firm pricing power and high capacity utilization, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives short-term S&P 500 outperformance over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns during late-cycle periods, or the signal fails to beat a random walk baseline over 100 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces market participants to price in aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when inflation crosses the 3.0% threshold, or the signal exhibits a Rank IC greater than 0.05 in the opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly scales forward corporate earnings expectations, creating a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions propagate through markets.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward equity returns over a 10-year sample, or the relationship reverses sign during standard economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm labor market tightness without triggering recession fears, fueling short-term risk-on equity flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative directional accuracy below 50% over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive power over individual series in a regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recoveries, housing starts act as a leading indicator for consumer spending and construction-related earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the housing cycle lifts broader economic activity.
failure_condition: Housing starts above 1800 units correlate negatively with 20-day forward returns during recovery periods, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 50 triggered events.
